key_insight: The paper proposes a domain-decomposed randomized neural network that uses different subnetworks for near-field and far-field regions to solve PDEs on unbounded domains efficiently.
engineering_relevance: This offers a practical mesh-free alternative for exterior-domain simulations, reducing truncation issues while preserving accuracy for localized features and far-field decay.
method: Assign separate randomized subnetworks to different spatial regimes.
Enforce coupling through boundary and interface conditions.
Solve only output-layer coefficients via linear least-squares using Petrov–Galerkin or collocation.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports numerical experiments on Poisson and time-dependent Schrödinger equations, showing accuracy and flexibility across semi-unbounded, fully unbounded, perforated, and time-dependent settings.
watch_item: Performance may depend on how well the domain decomposition, coupling conditions, and least-squares formulation are tuned for each PDE and geometry.
paper_title: Domain-Decomposed Randomized Neural Networks for Partial Differential Equations in Unbounded Domains
